info.description:
School Management System — Go + Gin + PostgreSQL

## Authentication
This API uses JWT Bearer tokens. Login via `POST /api/login` to receive an access token (1 hour) and a refresh token (7 days).
Click the **Authorize** button above and enter: `Bearer <your_token>`

## Roles
| Role    | Access                                              |
|---------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| Admin   | Full access to all endpoints                        |
| Teacher | Attendance, grades, locker (read public)             |
| Student | Own grades, report card, locker, finance             |
| Parent  | Children's data, report cards, finance               |
info.title: SMS Backend API

post.description:
Records or updates attendance for a student in a subject on a given date.
The teacher must be assigned to the subject being marked.
FIX #3a: The student must be enrolled in the subject — phantom attendance records are now rejected.

400.description: Validation error or student not enrolled in subject

get.summary: Get class attendance for a date (Teacher only)

get.description:
Returns overall and per-subject attendance stats for a student.
Accessible by: Teacher (any student), Student (own only), Admin, Parent (own child via ParentOwnsStudent middleware).

403.description: Forbidden — Students may only view their own attendance

post.description:
Records grades for multiple students in one call. Uses upsert — if a grade for the same
(student, subject, type, term, academic_year) already exists it is updated, not duplicated.
FIX #2: The upsert requires a DB-level unique constraint on (student_id, subject_id, type, term, academic_year).
Run migration.sql before deploying — without it the upsert silently creates duplicate rows.
FIX #3b: Each student_id in the grades array is now validated to be enrolled in the subject.

400.description: Validation error or student not enrolled

get.description:
Returns grades for a student filtered by term and/or academic year.
Accessible by: Teacher, Student (own only), Admin, Parent (own child).

403.description: Forbidden — Students may only view their own grades

get.summary: Get grades for a subject (Teacher only)

parameters.description: Grade type: Midterm | Final | Quiz | Assignment
parameters.name: type

get.description:
Returns a full report card with per-subject averages and letter grades.
FIX: now requires academic_year query param (defaults to current year).
Optionally filtered by term. Without these filters, cross-year averages were meaningless.
Accessible by: Teacher, Student (own only), Admin, Parent (own child via ParentOwnsStudent middleware).

parameters.description: Term filter: Term1 | Term2 | Term3 (omit for full year average)
parameters.name: term

403.description: Forbidden — Students may only view their own report card

get.description: Generates and streams a PDF report card. Accepts academic_year and term query params.

403.description: Forbidden — Students may only download their own report card

get.description:
FIX #5: SQL now uses NULLIF(COUNT, 0) and HAVING COUNT > 0 to prevent division-by-zero
for students who have no attendance records at all.

put.description:
Updates the homeroom teacher, year, or name of a class.
FIX: new endpoint — previously classes could only be created or archived.

delete.description: Blocked if any active students are still assigned to it.

post.summary: Enroll student in a subject (Admin only)

400.description: Validation error, student not found, or subject not found

post.description:
Creates a payroll record for a teacher for a specific month and year.
FIX: Year is now validated with min=2000 — previously 0 or 1 were accepted.

400.description: Validation error (including invalid year) or teacher not found

409.description: Payroll already exists for this month/year

patch.description: Sets a payroll record's status to Paid and records the payment timestamp.

Marks a pending receipt as Verified or Rejected. Can only be done once per receipt.
FIX #6: Admin note is only appended to the description when remarks is non-empty.
patch.summary: Verify or reject a bank receipt (Admin only)

get.description: Returns all files (public and private) for compliance or support review.
get.summary: List all locker files for a student (Admin only)

post.description:
Looks up all absences for a date (defaults to today) and sends ONE aggregated email per parent.
FIX #4: Parents with multiple absent children now receive correct per-child emails.
Previously the parent bundle only stored the last child's name — if two children were absent,
only one child's name appeared in the email. Now each child is tracked separately.
post.summary: Notify parents of absent students (Admin only)

post.description: Saves the notification, creates receipts for all targeted users, pushes live via SSE, and sends emails.

Creates a new user account. Requires Admin JWT.
Valid roles: Admin, Teacher, Student, Parent.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of all students. Use ?page=1&page_size=50.

post.description: Creates a new student account with a linked user login. The parent_id must be the ID of an existing User with role=Parent.

400.description: Validation error, invalid parent_id, or parent user is not role=Parent

409.description: Email or student code already exists

get.description: Returns a single student with user and class details.
get.summary: Get a student by ID (Admin only)

put.description: Updates class, parent info, date of birth, or parent_id for a student.

delete.description:
Deactivates the student's user account, soft-deletes the student record,
and removes all subject enrollments. Attendance, grade, and finance records are preserved.

put.description:
Updates a subject's name, code, or assigned teacher.
FIX: new endpoint — previously subjects could only be created or archived, leaving
orphaned teacher references after ArchiveTeacher with no way to reassign via the API.

delete.description: Blocked if grades or attendance records reference it.

409.description: Subject has existing grades or attendance records
Email or teacher code already exists

get.summary: Get a teacher by ID (Admin only)

put.description: Updates the qualification of a teacher and reloads the record before returning.

delete.description:
Deactivates the teacher's user account and soft-deletes the teacher record.
NOTE: subjects assigned to this teacher are NOT automatically reassigned.
Use PUT /api/admin/subjects/:id to reassign them before or after archiving.

Deletes the enrollment record. Attendance and grade records for this student/subject are preserved.
FIX: new endpoint — previously enrollments could only be created, never removed.
delete.summary: Remove a student from a subject (Admin only)

post.description:
Submits proof of payment via an Ethiopian bank receipt transaction ID.
Student can only submit for themselves. Parent can submit for any of their linked children.
post.summary: Submit a bank receipt (Student or Parent only)

403.description: Forbidden — Student/Parent only, or submitting for wrong student

get.description: Students see their own transactions. Parents see transactions for all their linked children.

delete.description: Removes the file from disk and the database. Student must own the file.

patch.description: Flips is_public on a locker file. When is_public=true, the student's teachers can read it.

get.description: Returns all files in the authenticated student's own locker (both public and private).
Returns only files the student has toggled to is_public=true.
get.summary: List a student's public locker files (Teacher only)

post.description:
Students upload files (max 5 MB) to their private digital locker.
Allowed types: pdf, jpg, jpeg, png, doc, docx, txt.
FIX #14: Both the file extension AND the actual file content (MIME sniffing) are validated.
A file with a renamed extension (e.g. shell.pdf containing PHP) is rejected.
FIX #15: Upload directory is configurable via UPLOAD_DIR env variable (defaults to ./uploads/locker).
post.summary: Upload a file to the locker (Student only)

400.description: File missing, too large, invalid extension, or MIME type mismatch

post.description:
Authenticates a user and returns an access token (1 hour) and a rotated refresh token (7 days).
Use the access_token in the Authorization header: `Bearer <token>`.
The refresh token is also set as an HttpOnly cookie (`sms_refresh`) for browser clients.
When the access_token expires, call POST /api/token/refresh.

Deletes the stored refresh token for this user and clears the sms_refresh cookie.
After logout, the access token remains valid until it expires (max 1 hour).

get.description: Returns the profile of the currently authenticated user.

put.description:
Updates the authenticated user's password. Requires the current password for verification.
Also revokes all existing refresh tokens (forces re-login on other devices).

get.description: Returns all notifications targeted at the current user, most recent first.

post.description:
Returns a 60-second token for use in the EventSource URL (?sse_token=...).
This avoids passing the long-lived access JWT in the query string (which would appear in logs).
The token is signed with a separate SSE secret and only valid for the /notifications/stream endpoint.

get.description:
Opens a Server-Sent Events stream. Receives a "notification" event for every broadcast targeting the caller's role.
FIX: This endpoint now uses SSEAuthMiddleware. Call POST /api/notifications/sse-token first to get a
short-lived 60-second token, then connect with ?sse_token=<token>.
The full access JWT must NOT be passed in the URL — it would be permanently logged by every proxy.

patch.description: Marks the notification receipt as read for the current user.

post.description:
Exchanges a valid refresh token for a new access token AND a new refresh token (rotation).
The old refresh token is invalidated on use (prevents replay attacks).
Token is read from the `sms_refresh` HttpOnly cookie (browser) or the JSON body (API clients).
The new refresh token is returned in both the response body and a new cookie.

parameters.description: Refresh token (API clients only — browser uses cookie)
parameters.name: body

body.example: Please collect report cards by Friday.

role.description: Valid roles: Admin, Teacher, Student, Parent

BearerAuth.description: Type "Bearer" followed by a space and your JWT token. Example: **Bearer eyJhbGci...**
BearerAuth.name: Authorization
